title: Procedure for measuring extraction efficiency
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ark0k","text":"Capturing dissolved organic matter from seawater using solid phase extraction is not 100% efficient. We use this protocol to quantify the amount of organic carbon retained during the extraction step. Comparing this value to the concentration of dissolved organic carbon in the initial water sample allows us to quantify the efficiency of the extraction procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ta4j","text":"Extract from solid phase extraction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"37gnb","text":"40 ml glass vials with Teflon-lined septa","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4pigt","text":"Concentrated hydrochloric acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tq29","text":"pipet and pipet tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9e3b5","text":"Vacufuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2q3k0","text":"TOC analyzer (our lab uses a Shimadzu TOC-VCSH total organic carbon analyzer with a TNM-1module for total nitrogen)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":43,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This protocol starts with the eluent from a solid phase extraction resin. Dry down eluents in 8 mL vials (all the way down, no solvent remaining!).
While drying down prepare 40 mL EPA vials with 25 mL MQ water and 25 µL 12 M HCl.
Once the 8 mL vials have dried down fully add 1 mL of MQ water and vortex.
Add 400 µL of this vortexed solution to your prepped 40 mL EPA vials. The final volume should be 25.425 mL .
Load the 40 mL vial on to the autosampler for the TOC analyzer ( 150 µL is injected onto the column).

Once these are done, dry down the remaining solution in the 8 mL vials for long term storage. Extraction efficiency calculation: Note that this calculation assumes that you have dried down the entire extract for this analysis and that no sample has been removed from the extract for other analyses. Example for 466 μM NPOC in DOC ( not TOC) sample and 240 ml total volume of extract: (240 ml) (466 μM) = (1 ml) (x) x = 111,840 μM in extract if 100% efficient Actual measured carbon in extract example (for sample with 46 μM measured carbon): (400 μl) (x) = (46 μM) (25 ml) (1000 μl / 1 ml) x = 2875 μM % efficiency = 2875 μM / 111,840 μM = < 3%
creator: Krista Longnecker
